claim: 84% of 128 lost outdoor-access cats were found within a five-house radius of their home (MPP 2013 internal data); Huang et al. 2018 formal study confirmed this finding.
stance_target: proximity as the highest-probability recovery zone for displaced pets
scope_conditions: Cats only; outdoor-access cats; US-based data; analogous dog study not yet published
quoted_support: 84% of 128 lost outdoor-access cats were found within a five-house radius of their home and... 92% of 158 cases of displaced, indoor-only cats who'd escaped outside were also found within a five-house radius of their home.

claim: As of Albrecht's writing, no formal 'Missing Dog Study' analogous to the Missing Cat Study has been published — the data gap for lost dog distances and search methods remains open.
stance_target: evidence gap — absence of peer-reviewed quantitative study on lost dog distance distributions
scope_conditions: State of published literature as of Albrecht's writing (pre-2026)
quoted_support: It is my hope that this information will encourage the development of professional and volunteer lost cat recovery teams... My next goal is to collaborate to conduct a much-needed 'Missing Dog Study' to learn more about the distances and most effective search methods used to recover lost dogs.
